title: T-033 — UI: TEC-011 Item Create Wizard modal (4-step)
prompt: Build the 4-step wizard modal (basic + classification + weight mode + extensions) per §6.5 TEC-011. Render as shadcn Dialog (matches prototype). Use RHF + Zod with schema fetched from server (T-027 cache). Steps: 1) Basics (item_code, name, type, uom_base); 2) Classification (product_group, gs1_gtin); 3) Weight mode (fixed/catch + variance fields gated by mode); 4) Extensions (L3 ext_jsonb fields). Submit calls POST /api/technical/items (T-009). Parity with prototype `product_create_modal` design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:22-136. RED tests/ui/tec-011-item-create.test.tsx. Run: `pnpm vitest run tests/ui/tec-011-item-create.test.tsx`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Item create wizard modal with 4 steps + RHF/Zod + dynamic extensions.
pipeline_inputs.details: Step navigation: Next/Back/Cancel buttons consistent with prototype. Weight-mode 'catch' reveals nominal_weight/gross_weight_max/variance_tolerance_pct. Use shadcn Dialog, Form, Input, Select, Switch.
acceptance_criteria: Given the modal opens, when compared to design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:22-136, then it has 4 steps in the same order, the same Next/Back/Cancel buttons, and uses the same shadcn primitives (Dialog, Form, Input, Select, Switch) — structural + visual + interaction parity verified by RTL test counting steps and asserting button labels.
Given weight_mode='catch' is selected, when on Step 3, then nominal_weight, gross_weight_max, variance_tolerance_pct fields appear and are required.
Given submission with duplicate item_code, when API returns 409, then form-level error 'V-TEC-01' shown.
Given user clicks Cancel, when on any step, then modal closes and no API call is made.
test_strategy: RED tests for parity (step count + button states) + Zod validation behaviour.
risk_red_lines: Do not skip validations between steps.
Do not hardcode extension fields — fetch via schema cache.
